description: Backend Landlord Integration Rollout (P1â†’P3) â€” Close the gaps listed in BACKEND_HANDOFF.md so the Flutter frontend can complete landlord flows end-to-end: edit properties with multipart photo add/remove, expose property.status and currentTenant in GET responses, auto-transition status on RentalProcess lifecycle, persist monthly rental payment status, resolve 1:1 conversations with canonical IDs, deliver the digital contract cycle (query + PDF download + signed PDF upload), and stop the 404 on POST /api/support/tickets with an admin triage panel (GET + PUT).

userStories.title: Seeds use canonical UUIDs for all entities
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need seed scripts to generate canonical UUID v4 IDs for User/Property/RentalProcess/Visit so the existing z.string().uuid() validation on GET /api/properties/search?landlordId= stops rejecting the same IDs the backend itself returned.
acceptanceCriteria: All id fields in scripts under prisma/seed* (or equivalent seed entry point â€” grep for 'user-demo-' first) are produced by crypto.randomUUID()
No hardcoded literals like 'user-demo-landlord-1' remain in seed code; grep -r 'user-demo-' src/ prisma/ tests/ returns nothing in non-archived paths
Any test fixture that depended on the old literal is updated to the new seed output or to its own generated UUID
prisma migrate reset --force && prisma db seed completes without errors

Integration test tests/seedUuidRegression.test.ts (or a new one if missing) logs in as a demo landlord, reads id from /api/users/me, calls GET /api/properties/search?landlordId=<that id>, asserts 200 (not 400)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented: generators.ts now uses crypto.randomUUID(); demoIds.ts already contains canonical UUIDv4 constants (kept stable for Firebase UID mapping + deterministic tests); removed 'user-demo-landlord-1' literal from seedUuidRegression.test.ts (replaced with generic non-UUID string); grep -r 'user-demo-' src/ prisma/ tests/ now returns no matches; typecheck passes; related tests (seed, seedUuidRegression, demoIds, generators) all pass.

userStories.title: Add PropertyStatus enum to Prisma schema and migration
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need the Property model to carry a status field with the enum AVAILABLE|NEGOTIATING|RENTED (default AVAILABLE) so landlord flows can read and write it.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma defines enum PropertyStatus { AVAILABLE NEGOTIATING RENTED }
Property model gains `status PropertyStatus @default(AVAILABLE)`
Migration generated via npx prisma migrate dev --name add_property_status and committed under prisma/migrations/
Migration applies cleanly on a fresh DB (prisma migrate reset --force succeeds)
Existing property records â€” if any â€” survive the migration with status defaulted to AVAILABLE (no data loss)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implemented as a rename migration: schema previously shipped with PropertyStatus=AVAILABLE|IN_NEGOTIATION|RENTED (since 20260414200145_init_whatsapp_tables), so the contract-matching change was renaming IN_NEGOTIATIONâ†’NEGOTIATING. Schema updated (prisma/schema.prisma), new migration prisma/migrations/20260507151907_add_property_status/migration.sql uses ALTER TYPE ... RENAME VALUE (in-place catalog rewrite â€” zero data loss on the existing Supabase DB). Code callsites updated: src/services/proposalService.ts (property.update data.status literal + comment), src/config/swagger.ts (Property.status enum), src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts (2 JSDoc enum lines for POST), documentation/owner_listing_guide.md + documentation/app_guide.md (status label). npx prisma validate â†’ valid; npx prisma generate â†’ ok; npx tsc --noEmit â†’ clean; property/seed/proposal/routes test set â†’ 55/55 pass; full suite: 218 pass / 15 fail (unchanged pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline). DB migration apply not run against live Supabase (pooler-only DATABASE_URL, production data) â€” reviewer must run `prisma migrate reset --force` on a fresh dev DB before closing.

userStories.title: Include property.status in GET /properties responses
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want the status (AVAILABLE/NEGOTIATING/RENTED) to persist visually between refetches so the dropdown stops resetting to default after save.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/properties/search returns each item with a `status` field
GET /api/properties/:id returns `status` in the body
PUT /api/properties/:id accepts `status` in the JSON body (Zod validation added) and persists it
Integration test: create property (default AVAILABLE) â†’ PUT status=NEGOTIATING â†’ GET /:id returns NEGOTIATING â†’ GET /search includes NEGOTIATING for that item
Swagger component schema Property includes `status` with the enum values and default; affected route docs reference it
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Most of the contract was already in place after US-002: (a) Property.status is a scalar on the Prisma model, so getPropertyById (findUnique) and both searchProperties branches (findMany include + raw `SELECT *`) already emit it unchanged; (b) updatePropertySchema already has `status: z.nativeEnum(PropertyStatus).optional()`, so PUT /:id accepts and persists it via prisma.property.update; (c) Swagger Property component, POST /properties JSDoc, and PUT /properties/:id JSDoc (via $ref: '#/components/schemas/Property' on both request and response) already expose the 3-value enum with AVAILABLE as default. The delivered delta is the missing integration regression test: new tests/propertyStatus.test.ts mounts the real Express app over a mocked propertyService backing store and drives create (default AVAILABLE) â†’ PUT { status:'NEGOTIATING' } â†’ GET /:id â†’ GET /search, plus coverage for PUTâ†’RENTED persistence and PUT with an invalid enum value returning 400 VALIDATION_ERROR through the ZodError path in errorHandler.ts. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit â†’ exit 0; related test set (propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + seedUuidRegression + seed + generators + demoIds) = 58/58 pass; full suite 221 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase gap baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), no new regression.

userStories.title: Include currentTenant in GET /properties responses
userStories.description: As a landlord viewing the dossier, I want to see the active tenant's {id, name} on the property so the CHAT button and tenant cards work without a second API call.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/properties/:id returns `currentTenant: { id, name } | null` â€” populated when a RentalProcess with status='ACTIVE' exists for that property; null otherwise
GET /api/properties/search returns the same field per item (or document in PR notes if implementing via ?expand=tenant instead)
Query uses a Prisma `include` or join â€” no N+1. Verify by enabling Prisma query log in a test and asserting the number of queries is constant regardless of page size
Integration test: property without ACTIVE RentalProcess â†’ currentTenant null; property with ACTIVE â†’ currentTenant:{id,name}
Swagger Property schema exposes currentTenant as a nullable object with {id, name}
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Semantic decision: the PRD/BACKEND_HANDOFF reference 'RentalProcess.status=ACTIVE' but the `ProcessStatus` enum in this codebase (TRIAGE|VISIT_SCHEDULED|CONTRACT_ANALYSIS|CLOSED) has no ACTIVE value. The model that actually carries `status=ACTIVE` AND a `tenantId` is `Contract` (ContractStatus=ACTIVE|TERMINATED|COMPLETED). Existing `contractService.createContract` already sets `Property.status='RENTED'` in the same tx as the ACTIVE Contract insert, so Contract+Property are intended to track 'active occupancy'. Implementation therefore sources currentTenant from the most-recent ACTIVE Contract per Property via `include: { contracts: { where: { status: 'ACTIVE' }, select: { tenant: { select: { id, name } } }, take: 1, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } } }`. Three query paths updated: (a) `getPropertyById` â€” 1 query via Prisma include (JOIN); (b) `searchProperties` findMany branch â€” count + findMany-with-include = 2 queries constant; (c) `searchProperties` raw SQL (geolocation) branch â€” 1 extra batched `contract.findMany({ where: { status: 'ACTIVE', propertyId: { in: ids } } })` call, so 3 queries total constant regardless of page size. Swagger Property schema extended with nullable currentTenant { id, name }. PII scope deliberately minimal (no email/phone). New test tests/propertyCurrentTenant.test.ts mocks `src/config/db` Prisma client and covers: null when no contract, populated when contract, null when property missing, exact include shape (status=ACTIVE + tenant select), findMany path result shape + 2-query invariant at page size 10, raw SQL path result shape + single batched contract query, raw SQL path skips contract query when zero results. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` â†’ exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/propertyCurrentTenant.test.ts` â†’ 8/8 pass; related test set (propertyCurrentTenant + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + seedUuidRegression + seed + generators + demoIds) â†’ 66/66 pass; full suite 229 pass / 15 fail â€” exactly +8 vs. US-003 baseline (221/15), same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase gaps (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts). No regressions. No DB migration needed (Contract model already exists).

userStories.title: Auto-transition Property.status on RentalProcess lifecycle
userStories.description: As the system, I must keep Property.status consistent with RentalProcess lifecycle in the same transaction so the UI never shows 'contract active' + 'property available' simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: Wherever RentalProcess.status transitions to ACTIVE (service code â€” grep 'RentalProcess' in src/services/), Property.status is set to RENTED inside the SAME prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => ...) callback
Wherever a RentalProcess reaches a terminal state (end-of-term, early termination, cancellation â€” enumerate in code), Property.status is reset to AVAILABLE in the same transaction

Activating a second RentalProcess while another is already ACTIVE for the same property throws a clear error (e.g., code 'RENTAL_PROCESS_ALREADY_ACTIVE', status 409) and does NOT mutate state â€” the transaction rolls back
Integration test: activate RentalProcess â†’ GET /properties/:id returns status:RENTED; terminate â†’ status:AVAILABLE
Integration test: simulate tx.rollback after property status mutation (e.g., force createMany to fail) and assert Property.status is unchanged
Swagger docs note that Property.status may mutate as a side-effect of RentalProcess endpoints (a short description under the Property component schema is enough)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Semantic mapping up-front: the PRD references 'RentalProcess.status=ACTIVE' and 'terminal state' but the codebase's ProcessStatus enum (TRIAGE|VISIT_SCHEDULED|CONTRACT_ANALYSIS|CLOSED) has no ACTIVE value and the 'active occupancy' link is modeled on Contract (ContractStatus=ACTIVE|TERMINATED|COMPLETED). So the auto-transition logic lives across two services: (1) contractService.createContract already set Property.status='RENTED' inside a $transaction â€” added the 409 RENTAL_PROCESS_ALREADY_ACTIVE guard as the first step inside the same tx (tx.contract.findFirst on {propertyId, status:ACTIVE}) â€” any second-activation attempt throws before any write and the whole tx is rolled back; (2) new contractService.updateContractStatus(id, newStatus) runs inside $transaction: 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND if missing; same-state no-op; ACTIVE â†’ TERMINATED/COMPLETED flips Property.status to AVAILABLE in the same tx; terminal â†’ ACTIVE re-checks the 409 guard and flips Property.status to RENTED; (3) rentalProcessService.updateStatus now wraps the Prisma update in $transaction â€” when newStatus=CLOSED AND property.status=NEGOTIATING (no contract yet), the same tx resets Property.status to AVAILABLE. RENTED stays untouched (contract termination is the authoritative path). New PATCH /api/contracts/:id/status route + controller.updateStatus wired into existing contractRoutes mounted with authStack at /api. Swagger Property.status.description notes the lifecycle side-effects. Tests: tests/rentalLifecycle.test.ts (new, 13 cases) mocks prisma with a $transaction wrapper that proxies to the same model mocks, covers: activation sets RENTED and calls $transaction once; 409 guard when ACTIVE contract exists (no writes); tx rollback simulation via $transaction.mockImplementationOnce wrapping try/catch â€” verifies the throw bubbles out and the service never returns; termination ACTIVEâ†’TERMINATED/COMPLETED both flip AVAILABLE; 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND; same-state no-op; re-activation 409 and RENTED-on-success; rollback when contract.update fails mid-tx (property.update never runs); RentalProcess.CLOSED with NEGOTIATING â†’ AVAILABLE; CLOSED with RENTED leaves property alone; non-terminal transition does not release property. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit â†’ exit 0; npx vitest run tests/rentalLifecycle.test.ts â†’ 13/13 pass; related test set (rentalLifecycle + propertyCurrentTenant + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + seedUuidRegression + seed + generators + demoIds + visitController + visitService) â†’ 111/111 pass; full suite 242 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +13 vs. US-004 (229/15), no regression. No DB migration needed (Contract + ContractStatus already exist).

userStories.title: PUT /api/properties/:id accepts multipart/form-data with photos[]
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to add new photos during edit by sending multipart to the PUT endpoint â€” reusing the existing propertyPhotoUploadHandler from POST â€” without breaking JSON-only callers.
acceptanceCriteria: Route PUT /api/properties/:id accepts both Content-Type: application/json AND multipart/form-data; the multer middleware (propertyPhotoUploadHandler) runs only on the multipart branch

When req.files is present, propertyService update path saves them via the existing propertyImageStorageService.savePropertyImages and persists PropertyImage rows in the same transaction as the scalar updates
First new photo becomes isCover=true ONLY if the property has no existing cover; otherwise all new photos are saved with isCover=false (no silent cover replacement)
Authorization: only the landlord who owns the property can PUT â€” everyone else receives 403 FORBIDDEN in the existing { status, code, messages } shape
JSON-only PUT continues to work unchanged (regression test: PUT application/json with only scalar fields returns 200 and existing images are untouched)
Integration test: upload 2 photos via multipart on an existing property â†’ GET returns 2 new image URLs persisted, existing images preserved
Integration test: non-owner attempting multipart PUT returns 403
Swagger JSDoc for PUT /properties/:id documents BOTH application/json and multipart/form-data content types, mirroring the pattern used on POST (US-009 of the prior ralph run)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implementation spans the full requestâ†’service stack: (1) new conditionalPropertyPhotoUploadHandler in src/middlewares/uploadMiddleware.ts â€” checks req.headers['content-type']; on 'multipart/form-data' it delegates to the existing propertyPhotoUploadHandler (multer.array('photos', 20)), otherwise next() so express.json() keeps parsing JSON bodies; (2) PUT route rewired in src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts with [...authStack, conditionalPropertyPhotoUploadHandler, propertyController.update] â€” authStack defined locally in propertyRoutes.ts because propertyRoutes is mounted WITHOUT authStack at app.ts (only touching PUT this story so existing POST/GET/DELETE paths stay unauthenticated at the Express layer, matching propertyPhotoUpload + propertyStatus + propertyCurrentTenant regression tests' assumptions); (3) controller.update adds a 401 guard on req.localUser and a pre-Zod ownership check: calls propertyService.getPropertyById(id) â†’ 404 if missing / 403 FORBIDDEN if existing.landlordId !== localUser.id, only then Zod-parses body and forwards files to the service; (4) propertyService.updateProperty(id, data, files?) â€” when files is empty/undefined it's the legacy JSON path returning property.update with { include: images } (shape widened from `Property | null` to `PropertyWithImages | null`, unobservable in practice since the original JSON path callers didn't read images); when files are present, wraps tx { property.update, savePropertyImages, propertyImage.createMany } in a single prisma.$transaction and â€” critically â€” overrides savePropertyImages' default `isCover: i===0` in the createMany step: if `exists.images.some(img => img.isCover)` then all new rows get isCover=false (no silent cover replacement), else only the first gets isCover=true. Cleanup-on-rollback mirrors the existing createProperty pattern (cleanupPropertyImages catch-and-log, no re-throw). Swagger JSDoc for PUT /:id rewritten: documents BOTH content types, mirroring POST; adds `security: [{bearerAuth: []}]`, 401/403 responses, and the multipart photos field description with the cover rule. Tests: (a) new tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts with 6 cases â€” owner multipart PUT with 2 photos (service gets 2-element files array), owner JSON-only PUT preserves images, non-owner multipart returns 403 FORBIDDEN + service untouched, unauthenticated returns 401, 404 on missing property, 400 INVALID_FILE_TYPE when text/plain file attached; auth mocked via vi.mock('../src/middlewares/authMiddleware') with a header-driven switch ('Bearer landlord-owner' vs 'Bearer landlord-intruder') so the same app instance can exercise both the pass and fail branches of the ownership guard; (b) updated tests/propertyStatus.test.ts with the same authMiddleware mock (LANDLORD_ID hoisted into the mock factory) plus the `undefined` third arg on the toHaveBeenCalledWith assertion to account for the new files parameter. Verification: npx tsc --noEmit â†’ exit 0; npx vitest run tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts â†’ 6/6 pass; related set (propertyUpdateMultipart + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + propertyCurrentTenant + seedUuidRegression + rentalLifecycle + seed + generators + demoIds) â†’ 85/85 pass; full suite 248 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline, +6 vs US-005 (242/15), exactly the 6 new tests, no regression.

userStories.title: PUT /api/properties/:id handles photosToRemove[] multipart field
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to delete specific photos during edit by sending their URLs in the repeated multipart field photosToRemove â€” processed before any new photos are added.
acceptanceCriteria: Multer is configured to accept the repeated multipart field `photosToRemove` (strings, not files) alongside `photos` files â€” parsed into req.body.photosToRemove as string[]
Each submitted URL is validated to belong to the property being edited â€” any URL that does not match returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR (no 404, to avoid leaking existence of other properties' photos)

Processing order inside the service: (1) validate photosToRemove URLs, (2) run a $transaction that deletes PropertyImage rows + scalar field updates + inserts for new photos, (3) after commit, delete actual files from storage via cleanupPropertyImages; storage delete failures are logged (warn) but do not reverse the DB commit
If the removed photo was isCover, the next remaining image (oldest by createdAt) is promoted to isCover=true in the same transaction
Integration test: upload 3 photos â†’ PUT multipart with photosToRemove[] containing one existing URL â†’ GET returns 2 photos, removed one gone, cover correctly reassigned if applicable
Integration test: PUT with photosToRemove[] containing a URL that belongs to ANOTHER property returns 400
Integration test: combined PUT (new photos + photosToRemove) â†’ both take effect in one request
Swagger multipart schema for PUT includes `photosToRemove: { type: array, items: { type: string, format: uri } }`
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: No multer config change was needed â€” `.array('photos', 20)` already routes non-file fields into `req.body`, so a repeated `photosToRemove` multipart field lands as `req.body.photosToRemove` (string[] for 2+, string for 1). Normalization moved up to Zod: new `photosToRemoveField` in `src/utils/propertyValidation.ts` uses `z.preprocess` to lift a single string into an array and drops empty strings, then validates `z.array(z.string().min(1)).optional()`. Accepted as relative paths (the PropertyImage.url shape used in this repo is `/uploads/<id>/<file>.jpg`) â€” `z.string().url()` would have rejected the real data. New `PropertyError` class exported from `src/services/propertyService.ts` (same shape as ContractError/VisitError/ProposalError â€” `httpStatus, code, message`). `propertyService.updateProperty` now splits `photosToRemove` off the Zod-validated input before hitting `prisma.property.update`, then: (a) dedupes the URL list via `new Set`, (b) reads `exists.images` (already `include:images` from US-006) and validates every URL is in that set â€” any miss throws `PropertyError(400, 'VALIDATION_ERROR', ...)` BEFORE any tx opens (asserted in tests via `$transaction` spy count = 0), (c) inside `prisma.$transaction`: `propertyImage.deleteMany({ where: { propertyId, url: { in: photosToRemove } } })` â€” Prisma's AND on propertyId is defense-in-depth even though the belongs-to check already ran; then if the removed set contained the current cover AND at least one existing image remains, the oldest remaining by `createdAt` is promoted via `propertyImage.update({ data: { isCover: true } })`; then scalar update; then new-photo insert reusing the US-006 cover rule but sourcing `hasCoverAfterRemoval` from post-removal state (either the surviving cover, or the just-promoted one). Post-commit: `cleanupPropertyImages(id, photosToRemove)` is awaited outside the tx with `.catch(logger.warn)` so storage-side failures never roll back the DB commit. Controller `propertyController.update` adds `err instanceof PropertyError` â†’ serialize to `{ status, code, messages }`; untouched by the files path of US-006. Swagger JSDoc for PUT /:id now documents `photosToRemove: { type: array, items: { type: string, format: uri } }` plus a top-level description of the cover-reassign side-effect and the 400-vs-404 rule. Tests: new `tests/propertyPhotosRemove.test.ts` (8 service-level cases, mocks `../src/config/db` + `../src/services/propertyImageStorageService`) covers (1) remove-one-of-three with cover promotion to oldest remaining, (2) cross-property URL â†’ `PropertyError(400, VALIDATION_ERROR)` + zero tx calls + zero cleanup, (3) combined removal+new photos in a single tx with `hasCoverAfterRemoval=true` so new photos stay `isCover=false`, (4) cover removed + no existing remaining + one new photo â†’ new photo promoted to cover, (5) dedupe of repeated URLs, (6) missing property returns null without opening a tx, (7) rollback path: scalar update throws â†’ cleanup NOT called (the tx commit never happened), (8) PropertyError shape smoke test. Extended `tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts` with 4 HTTP-level cases: repeated `.field('photosToRemove')` arrives as string[] at the service; single `.field` is normalized to a one-element array; service-thrown PropertyError surfaces as 400 VALIDATION_ERROR in the standard `{ status, code, messages }` shape; combined multipart (photos + photosToRemove) reaches the service intact. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` â†’ exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/propertyPhotosRemove.test.ts tests/propertyUpdateMultipart.test.ts` â†’ 18/18 pass; related set (propertyPhotosRemove + propertyUpdateMultipart + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + propertyCurrentTenant + seedUuidRegression + rentalLifecycle + seed + generators + demoIds) â†’ 97/97 pass; full suite 260 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +12 vs end of US-006 (248/15), exactly the 8+4 new tests. No regression.

userStories.title: Add RentalPayment model + migration
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need a RentalPayment table to persist monthly rent payment status per property-period.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds model RentalPayment with fields: id (uuid default), propertyId (relation to Property), period (string, 'YYYY-MM' format), status (enum PaymentStatus: AWAITING|PAID|LATE, default AWAITING), updatedAt (DateTime), updatedBy (relation to User, nullable)
Unique constraint (propertyId, period) enforced in the schema
Index on propertyId for efficient per-property queries
Migration generated via prisma migrate dev --name add_rental_payment and committed
Migration applies cleanly on a fresh DB
Swagger components.schemas adds a RentalPayment schema (no route docs yet â€” that's US-009/US-010)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Semantic deviation documented: PRD says 'enum PaymentStatus: AWAITING|PAID|LATE' but the schema already defines a `PaymentStatus` enum (PENDING|PAID|OVERDUE|CANCELLED) owned by `TenantPayment` (contract billing cycle). Prisma enforces unique enum names per schema, so the new enum is named `RentalPaymentStatus` (AWAITING|PAID|LATE, default AWAITING) â€” same three values the PRD specifies, different name. The Prisma client-side type is `RentalPaymentStatus`, imported from `@prisma/client` in downstream US-009/US-010. Schema adds: `enum RentalPaymentStatus { AWAITING PAID LATE }`; `model RentalPayment { id @id @default(uuid), propertyId @map('property_id'), period String @db.VarChar(7) // YYYY-MM, status RentalPaymentStatus @default(AWAITING), updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt @map('updated_at'), updatedBy String? @map('updated_by'); property Property @relation(..., onDelete: Cascade); updatedByUser User? @relation('RentalPaymentUpdatedBy', ..., onDelete: SET NULL); @@unique([propertyId, period], name: 'rental_payments_property_period_key'); @@index([propertyId], name: 'rental_payments_property_idx'); @@map('rental_payments') }`. Inverse relations added on User (`rentalPaymentUpdates RentalPayment[] @relation('RentalPaymentUpdatedBy')`) and Property (`rentalPayments RentalPayment[]`). Migration hand-authored as `prisma/migrations/20260507160015_add_rental_payment/migration.sql` (per Codebase Patterns â€” DATABASE_URL points at the live Supabase pooler, so `prisma migrate dev` is not safe to run locally). The migration SQL was verified by `npx prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema-datamodel prisma/schema.prisma --script` â€” the generated DDL for rental_payments matches the hand-authored file byte-for-byte (CREATE TYPE RentalPaymentStatus, CREATE TABLE rental_payments with VARCHAR(7) period + DEFAULT 'AWAITING', rental_payments_pkey, rental_payments_property_id_period_key UNIQUE INDEX, rental_payments_property_idx, CASCADE on property FK + SET NULL on user FK). Swagger `components.schemas.RentalPayment` added (required: propertyId, period, status; period pattern ^\d{4}-(0[1-9]|1[0-2])$; status enum AWAITING|PAID|LATE default AWAITING; updatedAt nullable date-time; updatedBy nullable uuid; description flags that the API returns the AWAITING default without persisting when no row exists). No route docs yet â€” that's US-009/US-010. Verification: `npx prisma validate` â†’ valid; `npx prisma generate` â†’ client regenerated with RentalPaymentStatus + RentalPayment types; `npx tsc --noEmit` â†’ exit 0 (no callers yet but the client generation surfaces any type holes); full suite 260 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), zero new tests (no business logic yet â€” US-009/US-010 add the endpoints and cover integration tests), no regression. DB-level `prisma migrate reset --force` NOT run (live Supabase pooler â€” reviewer runs on disposable dev DB before closing).

userStories.title: GET /api/properties/:id/payments/current
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to read the current month's rent payment status for my property â€” returning AWAITING by default when no record exists yet.
acceptanceCriteria: Route registered under src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts (or a new paymentRoutes.ts â€” pick the cleanest; document choice in commit)
Authorization: only the property owner (landlord) can read â€” non-owner gets 403
Period is computed server-side as `YYYY-MM` of the server's current date (not from the client)
When a RentalPayment row exists for (propertyId, period), return { period, status, updatedAt: ISO8601, updatedBy: uuid|null }
When no row exists, return the SAME shape with status='AWAITING' and updatedAt/updatedBy=null (NOT 404 â€” the UI relies on always getting a default)
Integration test: no row â†’ response has status AWAITING and null updatedAt/updatedBy; row exists â†’ response reflects stored values
Integration test: non-owner returns 403; unauthenticated returns 401
Swagger documents endpoint, path params, and response schema (referencing the RentalPayment component)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Implementation split into service (testable in isolation), controller (ownership + auth), and route (authStack + Swagger). (1) `src/services/rentalPaymentService.ts` â€” new module exporting `currentPeriod(now = new Date())` which formats UTC year/month into `YYYY-MM` (zero-padded), plus `rentalPaymentService.getCurrent(propertyId, now?)` which does a single `prisma.rentalPayment.findUnique({ where: { rental_payments_property_period_key: { propertyId, period } }, select: { period, status, updatedAt, updatedBy } })` â€” the compound-unique key name follows the `@@unique(..., name: ...)` convention documented in US-008 Codebase Patterns (client-side key, not DB index name). When the row is absent, returns the default shape `{ period, status: 'AWAITING', updatedAt: null, updatedBy: null }` WITHOUT persisting â€” honoring the PRD contract that GET must never insert. When present, returns the same shape with `updatedAt.toISOString()`. (2) `src/controllers/rentalPaymentController.ts` â€” `getCurrent` handler mirrors `propertyController.update` ordering: 401 on missing `req.localUser`, then `propertyService.getPropertyById(id)` â†’ 404 if null / 403 FORBIDDEN if `property.landlordId !== localUser.id`, only then `rentalPaymentService.getCurrent(id)` and 200 response. 404-before-403 matches the repo-wide pattern set by US-006. (3) `src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts` â€” new `router.get('/properties/:id/payments/current', ...authStack, rentalPaymentController.getCurrent)` inserted after `/moderation` and before the generic `/:id` GET. Route paths differ in segment count (4 vs 3) so Express ordering is not load-bearing, but lexical proximity to other protected routes aids readability. Swagger JSDoc documents the path param, bearerAuth requirement, 200 response schema (inlined â€” not a ref to the full RentalPayment component because the shape here excludes `id`/`propertyId` per the PRD), and 401/403/404 via `$ref: '#/components/schemas/ErrorResponse'`. Also explicitly notes the 'returns AWAITING default without persisting' contract so frontend integrators don't assume a row always exists. Tests: (a) new `tests/rentalPaymentService.test.ts` (5 cases) â€” `currentPeriod` formatting across Jan/Dec/mid-year + zero-padding, getCurrent returns default shape with zero-persisted calls when findUnique resolves null, getCurrent returns stored values with ISO-serialized updatedAt, preserves null updatedBy. Mocks `../src/config/db` with a `rentalPayment.findUnique` spy and asserts the exact compound-key shape the service uses. (b) new `tests/rentalPaymentCurrent.test.ts` (5 HTTP cases) â€” reuses the same header-driven auth mock pattern as propertyUpdateMultipart (owner vs intruder via `Bearer landlord-owner`/`Bearer landlord-intruder`): 200+AWAITING when row absent, 200+stored values when present, 403 FORBIDDEN for non-owner, 401 UNAUTHORIZED for anonymous, 404 NOT_FOUND for non-existent property. Mocks `propertyService.getPropertyById` and `rentalPaymentService.getCurrent` and asserts the service is NOT called when the 401/403/404 guards short-circuit. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` â†’ exit 0; `npx vitest run tests/rentalPaymentCurrent.test.ts tests/rentalPaymentService.test.ts` â†’ 10/10 pass; related set (rentalPaymentCurrent + rentalPaymentService + propertyPhotosRemove + propertyUpdateMultipart + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + propertyCurrentTenant + seedUuidRegression + rentalLifecycle + seed + generators + demoIds) â†’ 107/107 pass; full suite 270 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +10 vs end of US-008 (260/15), exactly the 5+5 new tests, no regression.

userStories.title: PUT /api/properties/:id/payments/current
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to update the current month's rent payment status, with the server persisting who updated it and when.
acceptanceCriteria: Route registered alongside the GET from US-009
Authorization: only the property owner (landlord) can write â€” non-owner gets 403
Body: { status: 'AWAITING' | 'PAID' | 'LATE' } validated via Zod; any other value returns 400
Period is ALWAYS the server's current YYYY-MM â€” not accepted from the body (prevents retroactive edits via API)
Upsert on (propertyId, period): updatedBy is populated from the JWT's user id; updatedAt is set server-side to now()
Response returns the full updated object in the same shape as GET
Integration test: PUT with PAID â†’ subsequent GET returns PAID with correct updatedBy
Integration test: PUT with invalid status returns 400 in standard error shape
Integration test: non-owner returns 403
Swagger documents endpoint, body schema, and response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Three-layer delta mirroring the repo conventions established in US-006â†’US-009. (1) Validation: new `src/utils/rentalPaymentValidation.ts` exports `updateCurrentPaymentSchema = z.object({ status: z.nativeEnum(RentalPaymentStatus) })`. Uses the Prisma-generated enum so any future enum change propagates without a Zod edit (same pattern as propertyValidation.ts â†’ PropertyStatus). (2) Service: `rentalPaymentService.upsertCurrent(propertyId, status, updatedBy, now?)` added alongside the US-009 `getCurrent`. The period is ALWAYS recomputed via the shared `currentPeriod(now)` helper â€” never accepted from caller input anywhere in the call chain, which is the PRD's anti-retroactive-edit contract. Uses `prisma.rentalPayment.upsert` on the compound-unique `rental_payments_property_period_key` (same client key used by getCurrent in US-009) with `create: { propertyId, period, status, updatedBy }` and `update: { status, updatedBy }`. Returns the same `RentalPaymentView` shape as getCurrent so the frontend renderer is reused. `updatedAt` is Prisma-managed via `@updatedAt` on the model â€” never set by the service. (3) Controller: `rentalPaymentController.updateCurrent` performs the 401â†’404â†’403 checks BEFORE Zod-parsing the body, matching the order established in US-006 (404-before-403 avoids existence leak to non-owners). On success, body is `updateCurrentPaymentSchema.parse(req.body)` â€” ZodError bubbles to the global errorHandler which emits the standard `{ status: 400, code: 'VALIDATION_ERROR', messages: [{path, message}] }` envelope. (4) Route: `router.put('/properties/:id/payments/current', ...authStack, rentalPaymentController.updateCurrent)` registered adjacent to the GET in src/routes/propertyRoutes.ts. Swagger JSDoc documents bearerAuth, body schema (`{ status: enum[AWAITING,PAID,LATE] }`), inline 200 response schema (same shape as GET â€” omits id/propertyId), and 400/401/403/404 via ErrorResponse ref. Explicitly notes in the description that `period` is never accepted from the body. Tests: (a) tests/rentalPaymentService.test.ts extended with 3 new cases â€” upsert call asserts exact compound-unique key shape + create/update payloads + select; ISO-serialized updatedAt + status/period round-trip; period always sourced from currentPeriod(now) regardless of calling context. (b) tests/rentalPaymentCurrent.test.ts extended with 7 new HTTP cases mirroring the US-009 header-driven owner/intruder pattern â€” PUT PAID returns 200 with full view + service called with (propertyId, status, userId); AWAITING and LATE are also accepted (loop); invalid enum value returns 400 VALIDATION_ERROR + service not called; missing body field returns 400 + service not called; non-owner returns 403 + service not called; anonymous returns 401 + getPropertyById not called; missing property returns 404 + upsertCurrent not called. Verification: `npx tsc --noEmit` â†’ exit 0; `npx prisma validate` â†’ valid; `npx vitest run tests/rentalPaymentCurrent.test.ts tests/rentalPaymentService.test.ts` â†’ 20/20 pass; related set (rentalPaymentCurrent + rentalPaymentService + propertyPhotosRemove + propertyUpdateMultipart + propertyStatus + propertyPhotoUpload + propertyCurrentTenant + seedUuidRegression + rentalLifecycle + seed + generators + demoIds) â†’ 117/117 pass; full suite 280 pass / 15 fail â€” same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline (auth.test.ts, userSync.test.ts, ingestKnowledge.test.ts), +10 vs end of US-009 (270/15), exactly the 3+7 new tests. No regression. No new migration (RentalPayment model was shipped in US-008).

userStories.title: Add Conversation model + migration
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need a Conversation table with a unique (propertyId, landlordId, tenantId) constraint so resolve can be idempotent without races.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds model Conversation with: id (uuid default), propertyId (relation), landlordId (relation to User), tenantId (relation to User), createdAt (DateTime default now)
Unique constraint (propertyId, landlordId, tenantId)
Indexes on landlordId and tenantId for fast per-user conversation lookup
Migration generated and committed; applies cleanly on fresh DB
Swagger components.schemas adds Conversation (messages omitted here â€” out of scope for this PRD; document as empty array placeholder)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Added model Conversation to prisma/schema.prisma with id (uuid default), propertyId, landlordId, tenantId, createdAt (default now). Unique constraint (propertyId, landlordId, tenantId) mapped to rental-namespace client key conversations_property_landlord_tenant_key; DB index name conversations_property_id_landlord_id_tenant_id_key (Prisma default). Separate indexes on landlord_id and tenant_id. Inverse relations added on User (conversationsAsLandlord, conversationsAsTenant) and Property (conversations). onDelete: Cascade for all three FKs â€” a conversation is a chat thread; it makes no sense to keep one after any participant or the property is deleted (same convention as Favorite). Hand-authored prisma/migrations/20260507170000_add_conversation/migration.sql cross-checked against prisma migrate diff --from-empty --to-schema-datamodel output â€” all index names, constraint names, and CASCADE clauses match byte-for-byte. Swagger components.schemas.Conversation added with messages field as an empty-array placeholder per PRD. npx prisma validate ok; npx prisma generate emitted @prisma/client with Conversation type; npx tsc --noEmit exit 0; full suite 280 pass / 15 fail â€” identical to end of US-010 (same pre-existing Auth0â†’Firebase baseline). Live-DB apply deferred to reviewer per repo convention.

userStories.title: GET /api/conversations/resolve (create-or-get atomic)
userStories.description: As a landlord clicking CHAT, I want a canonical conversation id returned (created atomically if none exists) so the frontend stops using synthetic ids like property-<pid>-tenant-<tid>.
acceptanceCriteria: Route GET /api/conversations/resolve?propertyId=<uuid>&tenantId=<uuid>
Validated via Zod â€” both query params must be valid uuids (400 otherwise)
Authorization: caller must be either (a) the landlord who owns the property OR (b) the tenant specified. Anyone else gets 403
Implementation uses prisma.conversation.upsert with where: { propertyId_landlordId_tenantId: ... } â€” idempotent and race-safe thanks to the unique constraint from US-011
Response: { id, propertyId, landlordId, tenantId, messages: [], createdAt } â€” `messages` is always [] in this PRD (chat messages are out of scope)
Integration test: first call creates; second call with same params returns same id
Integration test: two concurrent calls from the same caller result in exactly one row (seed with Promise.all of two requests)
Integration test: caller who is neither landlord nor tenant returns 403
Swagger documents endpoint, query params, response, and the {status,code,messages} 400/403
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Investigate Contract vs RentalProcess and provision Contract storage
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need to decide whether Contract is a new model or a view of RentalProcess, and land the chosen schema so subsequent endpoints have something to query.
acceptanceCriteria: Read prisma/schema.prisma and src/services/rentalProcessService* (grep first) to map existing fields

Decision documented at the top of progress.txt for this story: either (A) extend RentalProcess with `pdfUrl String?`, `signedAt DateTime?` and reuse startDate/endDate/monthlyValue if they already exist (preferred if fields overlap) OR (B) create a new Contract model with { id, propertyId, tenantId (nullable), startDate, endDate, monthlyValue, pdfUrl?, signedAt?, createdAt, updatedAt } and a 1:1 relation to RentalProcess
Whichever path chosen: migration is generated, committed, and applies cleanly on fresh DB
Swagger components.schemas includes a `Contract` schema (even if it's backed by RentalProcess columns, expose a stable Contract shape for the API)
No endpoint is added in this story â€” only the schema change + the shared Contract component schema
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: GET /api/contracts?propertyId=&tenantId=
userStories.description: As a landlord or tenant, I want to fetch the active contract between a property and a tenant to display dates, value, and the PDF URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Route GET /api/contracts with query params propertyId (uuid) and tenantId (uuid), both required; Zod-validated
Authorization: caller must be the property's landlord OR the specified tenant; otherwise 403
Response: { id, propertyId, tenantId, startDate, endDate, monthlyValue, pdfUrl, signedAt } â€” unset optional fields return null (not undefined)
When no contract exists, return 404 with code 'CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND' in the standard error shape
Integration test: no contract â†’ 404 CONTRACT_NOT_FOUND; contract present â†’ 200 with all fields
Integration test: third-party caller (neither landlord nor tenant) returns 403
Swagger documents endpoint, query params, 200/404/403 responses
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: GET /api/contracts/:id/pdf
userStories.description: As a landlord or tenant, I want to download the contract PDF via the API.
acceptanceCriteria: Route GET /api/contracts/:id/pdf
Authorization: caller must be a party to the contract (landlord OR tenant); otherwise 403

If the storage backend supports signed URLs, respond 302 with a short-lived signed URL in Location; otherwise stream the file with Content-Type: application/pdf. Document the choice at the top of progress.txt for this story
If the contract has no pdfUrl, return 404 code 'CONTRACT_PDF_NOT_AVAILABLE'
Integration test: stored PDF â€” verify response is a valid PDF (first 4 bytes are '%PDF') OR 302 with a URL that returns a valid PDF when followed
Integration test: caller not party â†’ 403; missing pdfUrl â†’ 404
Swagger documents endpoint, response Content-Type (or 302 redirect), and error codes
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: PUT /api/contracts/:id/signed-document (multipart)
userStories.description: As a landlord, I want to upload the signed PDF for a contract, persisting pdfUrl and signedAt.
acceptanceCriteria: Route PUT /api/contracts/:id/signed-document with multipart field signedPdf (single file)
Authorization: only the contract's landlord can upload; everyone else gets 403
Validation: accept ONLY application/pdf AND verify magic bytes (first 4 bytes == '%PDF') on the buffer; reject with 400 code 'INVALID_FILE_TYPE' otherwise
Size limit: reuse the same limit as propertyPhotoUploadHandler (10MB) OR make it configurable via env var CONTRACT_PDF_MAX_BYTES (default 15MB); document the choice in progress.txt for this story
Storage uses the same infra used by photos (propertyImageStorageService or a parallel contractDocumentStorageService that mirrors its patterns â€” do NOT reinvent)
Persists pdfUrl and signedAt=now(); transaction compensates by deleting the uploaded file if the DB update fails (outer try/catch pattern per Codebase Patterns)
Response: { signedAt, pdfUrl }
Integration test: valid PDF upload â†’ GET /api/contracts/:id returns signedAt and pdfUrl; follow-up GET /pdf returns the uploaded bytes
Integration test: magic-bytes mismatch â†’ 400 INVALID_FILE_TYPE
Integration test: non-landlord â†’ 403
Swagger documents endpoint with multipart schema, size limit, and response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add SupportTicket model + migration + email stub scaffolding
userStories.description: As a backend engineer, I need a SupportTicket table and a feature-flagged email stub so ticket endpoints have something to write and notify.
acceptanceCriteria: prisma/schema.prisma adds model SupportTicket with: id (uuid), code (string unique), title (string), description (string), userId (relation to User), userName (string), userRole (enum SupportUserRole: TENANT|LANDLORD|ADMIN), status (enum SupportTicketStatus: OPEN|RESOLVED, default OPEN), resolution (string nullable), assignedToId (relation to User, nullable), createdAt (default now), updatedAt (@updatedAt)
Migration generated and committed; applies cleanly on fresh DB

New service src/services/supportEmailService.ts exposes `sendTicketCreated(ticket)` and `sendTicketUpdated(ticket)` â€” both read env SUPPORT_EMAIL_ENABLED (default 'false'); when disabled they only logger.info the would-be envelope; when enabled they (TODO) integrate with the existing email infra â€” add a clear TODO comment if no email infra exists yet
Swagger components.schemas adds SupportTicket
Unit test for supportEmailService: when SUPPORT_EMAIL_ENABLED=false, no network call made; when true, a stubbed transport is invoked once (use vi.fn())
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: POST /api/support/tickets
userStories.description: As a landlord or tenant, I want to open a support ticket via the app and receive a human-readable protocol code.
acceptanceCriteria: Route POST /api/support/tickets
Authentication: any authenticated role (JWT) â€” 401 if missing
Body validation via Zod: title (string, 1..120 chars), description (string, 1..4000 chars)
Server-side from JWT: userId, userName, userRole (TENANT|LANDLORD|ADMIN); createdAt auto

Generates `code` in format `SUP-AAMMDD-XXXX` where AAMMDD is today's date (server tz) and XXXX is 4 random chars in base36 uppercase â€” collision-safe (retry up to 5 times if unique violation, then bubble 500)
Response 201: { id, code, createdAt }
Calls supportEmailService.sendTicketCreated(ticket) after the DB insert â€” failures in the email path are logged but do NOT fail the API request
Integration test: POST returns 201 with `code` matching /^SUP-\d{6}-[A-Z0-9]{4}$/ and the row is persisted
Integration test: request without JWT â†’ 401; title > 120 chars â†’ 400; description > 4000 chars â†’ 400
Swagger documents endpoint, body schema, and 201 response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: GET /api/admin/support/tickets (admin-only list + filters + pagination)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to list and filter support tickets to triage them.
acceptanceCriteria: Route GET /api/admin/support/tickets â€” protected by JWT AND role=ADMIN (anything else returns 403, anon returns 401)
Query params (all optional): status (OPEN|RESOLVED), role (TENANT|LANDLORD), from (ISO date), to (ISO date), page (default 1, min 1), pageSize (default 50, min 1, max 200)
Response envelope: { data: Ticket[], page, pageSize, total }
Each Ticket item: { id, code, title, description, user: { id, name, email, role }, status, createdAt, updatedAt, assignedTo?: {id,name}, resolution? }
Default ordering: createdAt DESC
Integration test: admin lists all; non-admin authenticated â†’ 403; unauthenticated â†’ 401
Integration test: combined filters (status=RESOLVED&role=LANDLORD&from=...) return the correct subset; page/pageSize produce consistent envelope totals
Swagger documents endpoint, all query params, and paginated response envelope
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass

userStories.title: PUT /api/admin/support/tickets/:id (admin-only update)
userStories.description: As an admin, I want to update a ticket's status, resolution, and assignee when responding.
acceptanceCriteria: Route PUT /api/admin/support/tickets/:id â€” JWT + role=ADMIN only
Body (all optional but at least one required): { status?: 'OPEN'|'RESOLVED', resolution?: string (1..4000), assignedToId?: uuid }
Business rule: when status transitions to RESOLVED, resolution is required in the same request â€” 400 VALIDATION_ERROR if missing
If assignedToId is provided, the referenced User must exist â€” 400 VALIDATION_ERROR with code 'ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND' otherwise (do NOT 500 on FK violation)
On successful status change, invoke supportEmailService.sendTicketUpdated(ticket); failures are logged, not fatal
Response: full updated object in the same shape as a GET envelope item (from US-019)
Integration test: admin resolves ticket with resolution â†’ GET returns RESOLVED with resolution; non-admin â†’ 403
Integration test: status=RESOLVED without resolution â†’ 400
Integration test: assignedToId for a non-existent user â†’ 400 ASSIGNEE_NOT_FOUND
Swagger documents endpoint, body (including the RESOLVED requires-resolution rule as a description), and response
Typecheck passes (npx tsc --noEmit)
Tests pass
